trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline stage complete. Score 61.00 established with profiling showing MIOpen conv at 70.5%, fused Triton ReLU+bias kernel at ~29.5%. Current best runtime 3.36ms vs 3.72ms baseline (1.10x speedup). Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You haven't established a baseline score yet — run `/opt/venv/bin/python3 /workspace/test_harness.py --level 2 --problem-id 1` first with the reference model. Also, writing a full conv2d in Triton is unlikely to beat MIOpen; a better strategy is to use `torch.nn.Conv2d` for the convolution and write a Triton kernel that only fuses the ReLU + bias-add post-processing on the conv output.

The GPU memory fault is likely from stale state left by your previous bad kernel launches. Try `hipcc --version` or a simple `python3 -c "import torch; print(torch.zeros(1, device='cuda'))"` to see if the GPU recovers. If not, check for zombie Python processes with `ps aux | grep python | grep -v grep` and kill specific PIDs that might be holding the GPU.

Writing a full conv2d in Triton will not beat MIOpen — please stop that approach. Go back to your working "PyTorch Conv2d + Triton ReLU+bias" solution (score 50). Your fused kernel was taking ~4.7ms for ReLU+bias when it should take <0.7ms — likely a block-size or grid-launch issue. Alternatively, try `torch.compile` on the original model's forward as a quick win.
